front: Poem: refrain "boomlay, boomlay, boomlay, BOOM" and warning "Mumbo-Jumbo will hoo-doo you"; includes performance directions like "with a philosophic pause"
Poem: declares "Death is an Elephant, / Torch-eyed and horrible"; describes a river "cutting through the jungle with a golden track"
Poem: a "bronzed, lank man" in a "suit of ancient black" paces near the old court-house, unable to sleep because "too many peasants fight"
Poem: celebrates the founder of the Salvation Army; repeats "Are you washed in the blood of the Lamb?"
back: "General William Booth Enters into Heaven" (Vachel Lindsay)
work: General William Booth Enters into Heaven

front: Author: wrote "Bryan, Bryan, Bryan, Bryan" calling William Jennings Bryan a "gigantic troubadour"
